title: To His Mistress.
content:
Choose me your valentine,
    Next let us marry -
    Love to the death will pine
    If we long tarry.

    Promise, and keep your vows,
    Or vow ye never -
    Love's doctrine disallows
    Troth-breakers ever.

    You have broke promise twice,
    Dear, to undo me,
    If you prove faithless thrice
    None then will woo ye.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To His Mistress.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
